channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: New Orleans barriers to prevent vehicle attacks were in the process of being replaced
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Seven years ago, New Orleans officials began installing adjustable barriers at intersections in the famed French Quarter to temporarily prevent vehicles from entering the tourist area where the narrow streets are typically teeming with pedestrians every night of the week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 1 person dies when truck catches fire and explodes outside Trump's Las Vegas hotel
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — One person died and seven more were injured Wednesday when a Tesla truck caught fire and exploded outside President-elect Donald Trump's Las Vegas hotel, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 1 dead, 7 injured in Tesla fire, explosion at Las Vegas Trump International Hotel on New Year's Day
item.description: A car fire at the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas caused smoke and loud booms, disrupting guests and nearby residents.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, NewsNation

item.title: Sugar Bowl postponed to Thursday night
item.description: Hours following the New Year's Day tragedy on Bourbon Street, Wednesday's Allstate Sugar Bowl bout between Notre Dame and Georgia is being postponed.
item.dc:creator: Richie Mills, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sugar Bowl in New Orleans postponed after deadly truck attack
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Sugar Bowl between Georgia and Notre Dame has been postponed after a truck plowed into a New Year’s crowd about a mile away early Wednesday, killing at least 10 people.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: The Latest: Investigators hunt for additional suspects in deadly New Orleans attack
item.description: A driver behind the wheel of a pickup truck rammed into a crowd of New Orleans revelers on Bourbon Street early on New Year’s Day, killing at least 10 people in what the FBI is investigating as an act of terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Family’s SUV stolen with senior dog inside
item.description: An Illinois family is pleading for answers and any information that could lead to the safe return of their beloved dog.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Spinelli, NewsNation

item.title: Commission to weigh offer to settle 'Jane Doe' suit involving Sean Williams
item.description: The city's legal department recommends "consideration and approval" in a brief item in the agenda packet.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Keeling, NewsNation

item.title: New York employers must now offer paid medical leave during pregnancy
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — Pregnant New Yorkers will be entitled to at least 20 hours of paid leave to attend prenatal medical appointments under a law that took effect Wednesday. Gov. Kathy Hochul said the policy makes New York the first state in the country to offer paid leave for prenatal care.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Broccoli sold at Walmart stores in 20 states could cause sickness: FDA
item.description: A fresh vegetables and salad company has issued a precautionary advisory for some washed and ready-to-eat broccoli florets distributed at Walmart stores in 20 states, including Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Protesters in Syria demand justice for disappeared activists and accountability from all factions
item.description: DOUMA, Syria (AP) — Protesters in Syria held a sit-in Wednesday demanding justice for four activists who were forcibly disappeared in 2013 and whose fate remains one of the most haunting mysteries of the country's 13-year civil war. On Dec.
item.dc:creator: OMAR ALBAM and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: L.A. County officials voting on measure to honor late former President Jimmy Carter
item.description: Los Angeles County officials are set to vote on a motion to honor former President Jimmy Carter, who died Sunday at the age of 100. L.A. County Board of Supervisors Chair Kathryn Barger and Supervisor Hilda Solis have introduced the motion to declare Thursday, Jan. 9, a countywide day of mourning.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: These farm animals will gladly munch on your old Christmas tree
item.description: You can drop off old Christmas trees at a Kent County farm where these four-stomached animals will munch on them.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, NewsNation

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, NewsNation

item.title: FBI finds 150 homemade bombs at Virginia home in one of the largest such seizures, prosecutors say
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Federal agents found one of the largest stockpiles of homemade explosives they have ever seized when they arrested a Virginia man on a firearms charge last month, according to a court filing by federal prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch a replay of the 2025 Rose Parade presented by Honda
item.description: LA's Very Own KTLA 5 is proud to offer live, nonstop coverage of the 136th Rose Parade presented by Honda on New Year’s Day, Jan. 1, 2025. Our broadcast and streaming coverage begins at 4 a.m. with an encore presentation of the 2024 Tournament of Roses Parade with grand marshal Audra McDonald.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, ABC 4

item.title: Driver 'hell-bent on carnage' kills 10, injures dozens in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A driver wrought carnage on New Orleans' famed French Quarter early on New Year's Day, killing 10 people as he steered around a police blockade and rammed a pickup truck into a crowd before being shot to death by police, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: New Orleans attack: Suspect identified, Sugar Bowl postponed
item.description: The FBI confirmed Shamsud Din Jabbar, 42, has been identified as the man who was shot and killed by police after the deadly New Year's Day attack.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Suspect identified after driver kills ten, injures dozens in Bourbon Street truck attack
item.description: At least 10 people are dead after a driver plowed a car into a crowd on Bourbon Street in New Orleans and then exited the vehicle and fired a weapon, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Multiple people injured in balloon-drop incident at Massachusetts arcade
item.description: Cheers of excitement quickly changed to horror when the netted balloons pulled a large display down onto the crowd below, including multiple children, Storyful reports.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Holiday travel warning: $5M worth of cars stolen from major US airport by organized theft ring, report says
item.description: An organized theft ring is accused of stealing nearly $5 million worth of vehicles from the Dallas-Fort Worth International Airport between 2023 and November 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Psychedelic therapy begins in Colorado, causing tension between conservatives and veterans
item.description: Colorado becomes the second state to allow psychedelic therapy this week after two years of rulemaking.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hundreds apply for restitution for abuse suffered at Florida reform schools
item.description: Hundreds of people who say they suffered physical or sexual abuse at two state-run reform schools in Florida are in line to receive tens of thousands of dollars in restitution from the state, after Florida lawmakers formally apologized for the horrors they endured as children more than 50 years ago.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Blake Lively sues 'It Ends With Us' director Justin Baldoni alleging harassment and smear campaign
item.description: Actor Blake Lively sued "It Ends With Us” director Justin Baldoni and several others tied to the romantic drama on Tuesday, alleging harassment and a coordinated campaign to attack her reputation for coming forward about her treatment on the set.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Nearly all of Puerto Rico is without power on New Year's Eve
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — A blackout hit nearly all of Puerto Rico early on Tuesday as the U.S. territory prepared to celebrate New Year’s, leaving more than 1.3 million clients in the dark. Officials said it could take up to two days to restore power.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Wild video shows moment brick display collapses onto crowd of kids, adults during NYE balloon drop gone wrong
item.description: Fire officials confirmed that 10 people were hurt when a brick display fell onto attendees at a family-oriented New Year's Eve event at a Peabody, Massachusetts, arcade on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New California law takes aim at homework burden on students
item.description: You may remember spending hours after school doing homework. A lawmaker in California is hope to change that for the next generation of students in the state.
item.dc:creator: Eytan Wallace, NewsNation

item.title: Multiple people injured in balloon-drop incident at Massachusetts arcade
item.description: Cheers of excitement quickly changed to horror when the netted balloons pulled a large display down onto the crowd below, including multiple children, Storyful reports.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: PlayStations for guns are offered up in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Dozens of cars lined up outside a New Orleans church parking lot this week as residents flocked to exchange firearms for video game consoles through a city-supported gun buyback initiative meant to improve public safety as Louisiana loosens gun ownership laws.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Have any New Year's resolutions? Here are some tips to help make them stick
item.description: Studies have shown that up to 70% of people who make New Year’s resolutions abandon those good intentions within months. Here are some tips for how to keep those resolutions when your willpower starts to falter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Angelina Jolie, Brad Pitt reach divorce settlement after 8 years
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Angelina Jolie and Brad Pitt have reached a divorce settlement, ending one of the longest and most contentious divorces in Hollywood history but not every legal issue between the two.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Southerners are cleaning up from tornadoes that swept the region
item.description: NEW CANEY, Texas (AP) — Cleanup was underway Monday after a strong storm system spawned hail, rain, high winds and tornadoes across the southern U.S. over the weekend, killing at least four people.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and LEKAN OYEKANMI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI issues warning after home of NBA star Luka Doncic targeted in string of burglaries
item.description: The FBI is warning sports leagues about crime organizations targeting professional athletes following a string of burglaries at the homes of prominent NFL and NBA players.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, KRON4

item.title: Census Bureau releases 'population clock' projection for New Year's Day
item.description: Based on current estimates, there will also be one birth every 9 seconds and one death every 9.4 seconds in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, WGN 9

item.title: These songs dominated the Billboard charts longest
item.description: Every now and then, a new song releases and dominates radio airwaves, Spotify playlists, and, of course, the esteemed Billboard Hot 100 chart—and in terms of those types of hits, 2024 has been pretty impressive.
item.dc:creator: Angela Underwood, Stacker, WGN 9
